text.title: Tailwind Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Tailwind Architecture methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: All design values MUST be defined in `tailwind.config.ts` (v3) or `@theme` block (v4); arbitrary Tailwind values (`w-[347px]`, `bg-[#3b82f6]`) are banned via ESLint outside safelisted one-offs.
rule.rationale: Arbitrary values bypass the token pipeline; on design change they require grep + replace and break IDE autocomplete.

rule.statement: Conditional class composition MUST go through a project `cn()` wrapping `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`; raw `clsx`-only joins are forbidden in lint.
rule.rationale: Without tailwind-merge, conflicting classes (`bg-red-500` + `bg-blue-500`) both apply and the last-in-string wins by CSS specificity - not by code order.

rule.statement: Duplicated utility chains MUST be extracted into a primitive ONLY when ALL of: >=5 utilities, >=3 call sites, AND represent a named UI concept (Card, Stack, Button); 2 duplicates are not enough.
rule.rationale: Premature extraction adds abstraction cost (imports, props, docs) without benefit; the 5/3 threshold makes it deterministic.

rule.statement: `@apply` MUST only appear in CSS files inside `@layer components`; `@apply` in component files (`.tsx`, `.vue`, `.svelte`) or `@layer utilities` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: @apply in components defeats JIT tree-shaking and inflates the CSS bundle; component abstraction serves the same purpose with better colocation.

rule.statement: Class order MUST be enforced by `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`; manual reordering in PR review is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent class order makes diffs unreadable and causes merge conflicts on unrelated lines; automated formatting removes the discussion.

rule.statement: When the project uses custom token utilities (e.g. `text-brand-*`), `cn()` MUST use `extendTailwindMerge` so conflicts are resolved correctly across the custom group.
rule.rationale: Stock tailwind-merge does not know custom utilities; conflicts in the custom group fall back to CSS specificity which is order-dependent.

rule.statement: Dark mode styles MUST be expressed via semantic tokens (e.g. `bg-background`, `text-foreground`); concrete `dark:bg-gray-800` is forbidden in production code.
rule.rationale: Concrete dark shades require per-component overrides and drift; semantic tokens propagate theme changes automatically.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
